description_long: This is a medium-sized brown frog with a slender, elongated body and long, muscular hind legs adapted for jumping. Its skin is mottled with dark brown spots and patches, providing camouflage against natural backgrounds. The frog has prominent, round eyes with horizontal pupils, and a smooth, moist skin texture typical of amphibians. Its forelimbs are shorter and end in delicate, webbed fingers, while the hind limbs are robust and end in long, webbed toes. The underside appears lighter in color, and the overall body shape is streamlined for efficient movement both on land and in water. The animal's size and proportions suggest it is a typical member of the Ranidae family, commonly found in urban parks and gardens.
description: A brown, mottled frog with long, powerful hind legs and smooth, moist skin, featuring prominent eyes and webbed toes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the frog's elongated body, muscular hind legs, and prominent eye.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the wide spread of the hind legs and the patterning on the back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the frog's body profile and the webbing of the toes.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the broad head, large eyes, and forelimbs positioned forward.
